items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Aha, you're finally here.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: It's been a while, my sweet seed of fate.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Are you here for the flowers? Perfect timing. They're about to bloom.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Consultants roam all across Solaris, and when they return, they always bring rare flower seeds as souvenirs.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Nah-uh, I just enjoy planting them.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Watching them grow, waiting for them to bloom—you know, waiting makes the fruits so much sweeter.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Of course, I have my own special seed.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Every day, I'm consumed by the urge to see it thrive, trembling with excitement for its bloom, the soft touch of petals unfolding in my palm... This feeling… I can't wait to make it mine!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hehe, it's the best thing ever to watch and protect it as its breeder.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Just a little fun of mine. Well, how's it going, {PlayerName}? Enjoying your time here?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: I heard you got the Shorekeeper and all of us out of a huge mess, huh?

options.text: If I remember correctly, you weren't here back then...

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hehe, I expected as much. You've shown great potential since you arrived in Jinzhou.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Well... I'm a Bloom Bearer, and a Bloom Bearer knows what they want to know.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Aww, you're a sharp one. Yes, I had something else going on, couldn't say hi to you first. What a pity.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Didn't know the Shorekeeper would entrust a newcomer with such important tasks... She trusts you. A lot.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: You are…

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Hey, {PlayerName}. It's Beatrice. Remember me from the Error Cell?

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: I hate to ask, but could you come back to the Error Cell?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What happened?

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: We've run into some trouble, and honestly, you're the only one the Shorekeeper trusts to handle it.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: I'll explain everything when you get here.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Thanks a lot! And don't worry, there'll be a reward for you.

options.text: Duty calls. I have to go.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: "Oh, congratulations, {Male=Mr.;Female=Ms.} Popularity!" Whatever…

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: If only you could be mine… I'd root you in my garden, where only I could savor your sweet, robust vitality… Hehe, just kidding. Hope you don't mind.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: …How strange.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: I've felt this way before…

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hehe, interesting.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: {PlayerName}, there you are! It's been a while, and you still look great.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Since you came to the Black Shores, so many unexpected things have happened… Even the Shorekeeper made an appearance.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Anyway, let's get to the point. Do you remember what the Error Cell is for?

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Great, your memory is as sharp as ever.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Well, it's basically a "prison" for containing hazardous waste like corrupted Stellar Matrices.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: The problem is, one of the "prisoners" has escaped.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: It's headed towards the Necrostar and seems to be trying to go even deeper.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: The Shorekeeper is tied up with modulation and can't leave.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: That's why I need your help to recapture it and prevent any more incidents. You're the best person for the job.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Don't worry, I'll make it worth your time.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: The escapee is hiding, but we can use the remnants of its data to track it down.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (…I think I heard something.)

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: What's the matter?

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: While investigating, I discovered that a few years ago, a Consultant accessed these data.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: She was out in the field recently and just returned to the Black Shores. You should check in with her first.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Alright. I'll call if I get anything.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Thank you. I'm counting on you!

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Hello, {PlayerName}, what brings you here?

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Oh? But Lady Flora's item was already picked up by another Bloom Bearer.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Who took it?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: You looking for me?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hehe, if you want it, try to catch me!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Oh, you're quick! I knew you wouldn't keep me waiting.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Were you following me?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Yeah, is that a problem?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: I followed you to the cell on a whim. Then I heard something interesting.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Plus, this thing is just finding its way back to its owner. I see no wrong in that.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: To its owner… Are you that "Lady Flora"?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Yes and no.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Just like you, I've completely forgotten about the past! All of it! So, I guess I'm not the Lady Flora you're looking for.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Of course, I did some digging, but… a little accident stopped me.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (If Camellya is Lady Flora, then we were supposed to be close, but neither of us remembers…)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The fact that I used to be the leader... This should only be known to the Shorekeeper.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Maybe I shouldn't mention it to Camellya until I'm sure she's not a threat.)

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: {PlayerName}, if you want to use that trinket to find the problematic matrix, then play with me! Entertain me, and I'll help you.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Well… Then I'll take this and make a run for it!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: You can try to catch me, but be warned, it won't be an easy chase. Or, you could play along and maybe I'll let you have it. It'll be more fun that way!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Since you handled the Threnodian, a little game of battle shouldn't be too much trouble for you, right?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: As a Bloom Bearer, I need to pick the perfect seeds for the Black Shores and give them my utmost care.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: All these years, I haven't met anyone intriguing—not one person!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: You're MY seed of fate. If I let you go so easily, all my efforts, my yearning, my hunger will not be satisfied.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: If you get taken or killed by somebody else...

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: ...I would be very, very, very unhappy.

options.text: Why are you so obsessed with me?
Why do you call me your seed of fate?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Why? It's pure instinct!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Flowers thirst for water; beasts hunger for flesh. It's nature, just like my craving for you.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: I want it, and I get it. That's how I live.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: That fleeing Stellar Matrix might affect the Necrostar’s functioning. Worse, it could threaten the Black Shores.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: So? I don't care! If this island's gonna blow up, so be it. Unless, of course, it ruins my fun.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Are you angry? Hehe, your anger is sooo delicious…

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Fight me. I can't wait to see who'll be the winner. I've been itching to get a taste of your blade.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hahahaha! Fantastic! Let's get started!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: What a show. You never disappoint me. With you around, the days ahead will be so much fun.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: The game's over. Here you go, the thing I promised you. Let's keep going, my hero~

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The info in that matrix is related to you. With you by my side, I should be able to locate and capture it faster.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hehe, alright. So...?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: We need to solve this accident as soon as possible, and you promised to help if I played the game with you.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Haha! Guess I have no other choice, huh?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: ...Well, it's lovely to see you being a little pushy too! I like your serious side. Sure, I'll go.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What else do you know about Lady Flora? You said a small accident happened during your investigation...

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Yeah, ended up stuck in a boring medical pod for half a month and almost died from severe Overclocking.

options.text: You call that a small accident?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Sure, it's no big deal, since I survived.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hmm... My memories were sealed by Lady Flora somehow. No matter how much I try to recall, it doesn't work.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: I only remember waking up in a cave, my mind completely blank. Drifted aimlessly for years, until I stumbled upon the Black Shores.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: And guess what? Turns out I had lived here before! Naturally, I wanted to investigate further and figure it out.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: So that's what I did. I'm sure I uncovered the truth behind my memory loss, but… when I woke up in the medical pod again, I'd forgotten everything.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Guess somehow I almost died from Overclocking when I learned about my past, and I had to seal my memories away again. That's pretty much what happened, I think?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: I still don't know why Lady Flora did that though... Well, I don't really care. I can have fun with or without those memories.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: It doesn't sound like you're talking about your past self.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hello~? Are you listening? Told you I remember nothing, so... Lady Flora is more like a stranger to me.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: It's like watching a movie. No matter how happy or sad the story is, it's someone else's story, after all.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Don't you feel the same? Tell me, what was on your mind when you witnessed those stories, staring at every frame on the screen?

options.text: ...I had too many things to think about.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Haha, you don't care, do you? I knew we think alike.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Aww... You are more sentimental than I expected.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Anyway, I'll trust my instincts over some distant memories.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: As long as my instincts remain, I'm still "me."

options.text: That sounds a bit too animalistic…
So you let your instincts define what's truly "you"?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Yeah, and what's wrong with that?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: That's right!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: My instincts rule me, and I rule them. I feel great joy when they are fulfilled, and when I ignore them, I suffer in return.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: They guide me like how duty and responsiblity guides you... Who says one is better than the other?

options.text: No, there is no difference between these two.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hehe, let's drop this boring discussion. Shall we split up to find the other stuff?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Sure. Whoever finds it first will go meet up with the other.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Ooh, then it's a competition—I'm in! Let the search begin!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: This Blake Bloom is…

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: {PlayerName}, I'm sorry. That matrix is more cunning than we anticipated… My mistake.

options.text: Do you know where she is?

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Camellya is still inside that Stellar Matrix.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: She was trapped inside and started to sink deeper—that's why you couldn't go any further.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Besides, Camellya has found the matrix core. Without her, it might have escaped again.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Now, we'll create a symmetrical Stellar Matrix, and that will allow you to reach the previously inaccessible areas.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: There will be various simulated areas. To reach the destination, you need to <color=Highlight>find and jump to the correct timeline junctures</color>.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Due to a mutated Stellar Matrix, you may not reach the destination in one go.

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: <color=Highlight>Multiple jumps</color> might be required to get there. {PlayerName}, please stay safe!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: {PlayerName}, do you still have that bracelet?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Good. You can keep it, so... a piece of me always stays with you.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Since the past between us is a secret now, consider this bracelet our witness.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What exactly is this bracelet…?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hehe, it's an old one. It was… a gift.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Got it for monitoring my Rabelle spectrum, as an Overclocking warning device.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: But I don't really need it anymore. Told you I'll never lose control again.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Except in a special situation like this, in most cases, nothing can even leave a scratch on me.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: I hit jackpot finding the matrix. It was a blast! But that's all in the past now.

options.text: You aren't going to get your memories back?
You said your memories are sealed by Lady Flora…

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: No, I'm quite happy with things as they are right now. No need to risk opening the floodgates and winding up in another medical pod.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Since Lady Flora—the past me—chose to seal away those memories, I'm fully behind my decision.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: As for the memories we used to share, if fate decides to reveal them, they'll resurface naturally as we continue our journey together.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Plus… does it really matter? No, it doesn't!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Fate has already brought us back together.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: I bet we'll have more wild stories to share, and more games to play.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: No point dwelling on the past. I'd rather live in the moment.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: …Speaking of which, I have a question for you.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: {PlayerName}… Are you happy now? Having lost all memories, living a new life...

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Is that so? Good to know. I'm happy too. Every moment <ano=Sparring>dancing</ano> with you brings me immense joy!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Then... If one day you get tired of all this saving-the-world stuff, I'll whisk you away to a place where no one can find us!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Heh, same here. But you'll be fine, because you have me now. We can take our time figuring out this life, together!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: This is where I first set foot on this island, where everything began. The stars lie beneath our feet, where the Tethys System resides.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Tethys records all data on Solaris, capturing countless possibilities born and fading away.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: But in every possible scenario, we always find each other.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: All paths in the stars converge towards this single endpoint, so—

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: The universes where we didn't meet must have all perished! Only this one survived!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: If the universe were a flower, then yes…

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: ...This must be the perfect world for a gardener like me.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Ah... Wonderful…

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: The way you fought really makes my heart race… Such power, such vitality…

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Ugh, I wish I were the one dancing this deadly, bloody, beautiful waltz with you…

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: …I wanted to help you up.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Hehe, I know. I was just overwhelmed with joy… Cough! Ugh…

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You're on the edge of Overclocking. And you were in a medical pod before... Does this happen often?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Yeah. Oh, are you worried about me?

options.text: …I'm just worried that you might endanger the Black Shores.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Aww, you're sooo sweet. I'm almost blushing.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: That's not considerate, darling. Besides, with the Tethys System here, I can't really cause any major damage.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: And I'm good at dancing on the edge. I won't let myself Overclock that easily. But if it does happen… I'll just accept it as my fate.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: It's very dangerous. You know the risks, right?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Of course I do. But once severe Overclocking symptoms appear, they can't be cured, can they?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: And I'm happy!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: It's been a blast competing and taking down those Tacet Discords with you... That's all I need to have a good time.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: If one day I die from Overclocking, so be it! The Black Shores will make sure there's no Tacet Field generated from me.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: I've always followed my instincts, ignored anything that didn't interest me. No regrets, even if I died now.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Carpe diem, enjoy the moment… Life is boring if you're stuck in the past or consumed by the future.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Anyway, what have you found? I got a stack of damaged photos. To locate that Stellar Matrix, we'll need three or four items tops.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: The remaining frequencies... Yes, it belongs to Lady Flora.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: It's been almost 20 years since the last frequencies were recorded.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Blake Blooms represent a Bloom Bearer's identity. They always have one on them. These frequencies can help us track Lady Flora's last activity.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: She and the leader of Black Shores vanished at the same time, no longer appearing in records.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Around the same time? The two cases might be connected...)

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Oh, right. I just remembered what that strange feeling I had earlier was.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: When I saw you in Jinzhou... It felt as if I'd seen you somewhere else before.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Anyway, have you told Beatrice that we're ready?

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: How impressive, {PlayerName}! That's fast!

items.speaker: Beatrice
items.text: Please head to the Modulation Hall. I'll meet you there.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: {PlayerName}, stay focused. Don't take your eyes off me!

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: When I found those photos, I made a decision.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Photos are great for capturing memories. Take some photos of me.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: In the past, others recorded us, but now we'll have to do it ourselves.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Consider it... a tangible keepsake.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: They last longer than our memories. A moment frozen in frame.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: As the Black Shores leader, a small task like this would be a piece of cake for you, I bet?

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Looks good. I wanna keep a copy of this too.

items.speaker: Camellya
items.text: Excellent composition. Quite the photographer, aren't you? Hehe…
